name: Montealegre del Castillo by Alpera — circular from Almansa
name_original: Montealegre del Castillo por Alpera — circular desde Almansa
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Almansa traverse a varied terrain, situated within a natural passage between mountain systems. The region features a blend of wide horizons, pale fields, and open skies, with notable natural features including the Sierra de Almansa, Sierra de la Oliva, and the Sierra del Mugrón. The landscape also incorporates the historic Almansa Reservoir and the prominent white limestone crag upon which Almansa Castle stands. These routes often feature well-paved surfaces, suitable for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Almansa The most popular touring cycling route is Montealegre del Castillo by Alpera — circular from Almansa, a 49.4 miles (79.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 55 minutes to complete. This route passes through varied landscapes and historical sites, including the Almansa Castle. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Palace of the Counts of Cirat – Castle of Almansa loop from Almansa, a difficult 24.8 miles (39.9 km) path. This route loops around historical sites such as the Almansa Castle and the Palace of the Counts of Cirat. Local touring cyclists also love the Hermitage of San Antón loop from Almansa, a 16.6 miles (26.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the surrounding plains, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Almansa is defined by diverse landscapes, mountain ranges, and historical sites, including the Almansa Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Almansa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almansa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Almansa?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes available around Almansa on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Almansa?
A: The region around Almansa features a varied terrain, situated within a natural passage between mountain systems. You'll find a blend of wide horizons, pale fields, and open skies, with many routes featuring well-paved surfaces suitable for touring cyclists. Some routes may include elevation gains, requiring good fitness.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Almansa?
A: Yes, Almansa offers routes suitable for various fitness levels. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are at least 4 easy touring cycling routes available. These typically feature less elevation gain and are a great way to explore the area at a relaxed pace.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling around Almansa?
A: The touring cycling routes in Almansa showcase diverse natural beauty. You can expect to see mountain ranges like the Sierra de Almansa, Sierra de la Oliva, and Sierra del Mugrón. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4071663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almansa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another prominent feature, offering scenic views. The landscape is characterized by wide horizons and open skies, especially around viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4713383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Museum Trail&lt;/a&gt; near the reservoir.

Q: What historical landmarks can I visit along the cycling routes in Almansa?
A: Almansa's routes often pass by significant historical sites. The prominent 14th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Almansa&lt;/a&gt; is a frequent sight, dominating the city from its limestone crag. You might also encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5674312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Assumption&lt;/a&gt; and the Renaissance-style &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5674298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of the Counts of Cirat&lt;/a&gt;. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068342932/montealegre-del-castillo-by-alpera-circular-from-almansa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montealegre del Castillo by Alpera — circular from Almansa&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Almansa Castle, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of the Counts of Cirat – Castle of Almansa loop from Almansa&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights these two landmarks.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Almansa?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Almansa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068342932/montealegre-del-castillo-by-alpera-circular-from-almansa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montealegre del Castillo by Alpera — circular from Almansa&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of the Counts of Cirat – Castle of Almansa loop from Almansa&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31518011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wildfire – Castle of Almansa loop from Almansa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Almansa?
A: The touring cycling routes in Almansa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of mountain views with open fields, and the historical sites like the Almansa Castle that are integrated into the routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Almansa?
A: Almansa generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are typically mild, making cycling possible, though you should check local weather forecasts for specific conditions.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Almansa?
A: While many routes involve moderate to difficult sections, there are easy routes available that could be suitable for families, depending on their experience level. These routes typically have less elevation and are on well-maintained surfaces. Always check the route details and difficulty before heading out with children.

Q: Is public transport available to reach cycling trailheads outside of Almansa town?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads outside Almansa town may be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to a starting point, especially for routes that venture into areas like the Sierra del Mugrón or around the Almansa Reservoir. Many routes conveniently start directly from Almansa itself.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Almansa?
A: In Almansa town, you can typically find parking in municipal car parks or on-street parking, especially near the town center or residential areas, which are often convenient starting points for many routes. For routes that begin further afield, look for designated parking areas near popular attractions or trail access points.

Q: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the touring cycling routes in Almansa?
A: While specific cafes directly on every route might be scarce, many routes either start or pass through Almansa town or other smaller villages. Almansa town center offers a good selection of cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's always a good idea to carry snacks and water, especially on longer routes.

Q: Do I need any special permits for touring cycling in the Almansa region?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for touring cycling on public roads and established trails in the Almansa region. However, if you plan to cycle through private land or protected natural areas, it's always best to check local regulations or signage. Stick to marked paths to ensure you're compliant with local guidelines.
tips:
The original construction could have been the work of the Almohads, who used to use the rammed earth technique for their defensive buildings.
The remains of rammed earth walls in this castle, traditionally attributed to the Almohad period, were actually built during the first decades of Christian rule, at the end of the 13th century or the beginning of the 14th century.
In the 14th century, Almansa and its fortress were incorporated into the Villena manor. Don Juan Manuel took advantage of the previous construction and ordered its walls to be rebuilt.
It is the refuge for shepherds and farmers typical of the area. It is in perfect condition and can be a good place to take shelter from bad weather.
It is a natural area characterized by the centuries-old Holm oaks that make it up. There are some picnic tables, swings for the little ones and a disused swimming pool.
